global_rules: This successor pack begins after Gates 51–58 and exists to turn the broad exploratory modification ideas into genuinely executable slices.
The binding cognition grammar order remains authoritative; no gate may reorder runtime stages unless a dedicated gate proves and documents the change.
One branch per gate; complete every planned leaf for the gate, pass targeted validation, merge to main, and only then open the next gate branch.
No leaf may silently introduce ad hoc event classes, calendar facts, precursor markets, or modifier surfaces outside the authority docs created in earlier gates.
Live paper remains a falsification/promotion surface for locked candidates, not an in-place coefficient-discovery surface.
State-conditioned modifiers are bounded runtime policies, not informal retuning; prohibited variable surfaces remain fixed.
Any gate that reveals ontology failure or a missing module must emit a research-reset note rather than smuggling the redesign into the current gate.

leaves.title: Freeze canonical event-class taxonomy
leaves.scope: Define the top-level event classes the desk runtime recognises and prohibit free-text runtime categories.
implementation_tasks: Write the canonical event-class list for company, macro, policy, venue, expiry, and exceptional desk-relevant events.
State explicit inclusion/exclusion examples so event classes do not drift by analogy.
Record forbidden free-text and overlapping categories that must not reappear downstream.
validation_tasks: Check the class list against docs/01_NORMATIVE.md, docs/02_OPERATING_MODEL.md, and the current events schema for naming conflicts.
Ensure the taxonomy is exhaustive enough for the known desk use-cases raised in the planning thread.
leaves.pass_gate: Freeze canonical event-class taxonomy is explicit, bounded, and consistent with the governing cognitive-workflow tranche rules.
leaves.merge_gate: Gate 59 remains a bounded implementation slice and does not silently absorb downstream gates.

leaves.title: Separate known risk, priced risk, and realised reaction semantics
leaves.scope: Pin the conceptual distinction between scheduled/known events, market-priced event posture, and realised post-event path.
implementation_tasks: Write precise definitions for known risk, priced risk, and realised reaction.
State which runtime stages may consume each concept and which stages must not collapse them into one label.
Give trader-style examples where two days look similarly nervous but differ in causal event class.
validation_tasks: Confirm the definitions do not contradict the seven-stage grammar or temporal/event ownership notes.
leaves.pass_gate: Separate known risk, priced risk, and realised reaction semantics is explicit, bounded, and consistent with the governing cognitive-workflow tranche rules.
leaves.merge_gate: Gate 59 remains a bounded implementation slice and does not silently absorb downstream gates.

validation_tasks: Check that impact tiers can be represented without new free-text fields.
leaves.pass_gate: Define event impact-tier vocabulary and examples is explicit, bounded, and consistent with the governing cognitive-workflow tranche rules.
leaves.merge_gate: Gate 59 remains a bounded implementation slice and does not silently absorb downstream gates.
